definition: Hiding implementation complexity behind a simpler interface to reduce cognitive overhead.
exampleSentence: The abstraction hides the fetch logic so components don't need to know the URL.

definition: Application Programming Interface — a contract defining how software components communicate.
exampleSentence: The frontend talks to the API to get data instead of hitting the database directly.

definition: Operations that run independently without blocking the main thread while waiting to complete.
exampleSentence: Fetching user data is asynchronous so the UI doesn't freeze while waiting.

definition: A single output file combining multiple source modules for efficient browser delivery.
exampleSentence: The production bundle is about half the size of the development one.

definition: A tool that compiles and combines source files into optimized assets for production.
exampleSentence: The bundler compiles TypeScript, resolves imports, and outputs a single JS file.

definition: A layer that stores frequently accessed data to reduce redundant fetching or computation.
exampleSentence: The request goes through a cache so the same data isn't fetched twice.

definition: A function passed as an argument to another function, invoked after an operation completes.
exampleSentence: Pass a callback to the button so it knows what to do when clicked.

definition: Content Delivery Network — a distributed server system delivering assets from locations near the user.
exampleSentence: Static assets are served from a CDN so they load fast anywhere in the world.

definition: Code or logic that executes in the user's browser rather than on the server.
exampleSentence: Form validation runs client-side first so users get instant feedback.

definition: A function that retains access to its outer scope's variables even after that scope has returned.
exampleSentence: The event handler closes over the count variable from the outer function.

definition: Dividing a bundle into smaller chunks loaded on demand to improve initial load time.
exampleSentence: Code splitting cut the initial bundle size by forty percent.

definition: A self-contained, reusable piece of UI that manages its own structure, style, and behavior.
exampleSentence: Each component owns its own state, styles, and behavior.

definition: A React component whose input value is driven entirely by state rather than the DOM.
exampleSentence: A controlled component gives you full control over what the input displays.

definition: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing — a browser security mechanism controlling cross-domain requests.
exampleSentence: The browser blocked the request because of a CORS policy on the server.

definition: The algorithm CSS uses to resolve which styles apply when multiple rules target the same element.
exampleSentence: Understanding the cascade explains why your styles are sometimes overridden.

definition: A reusable React function prefixed with 'use' that encapsulates stateful logic for sharing.
exampleSentence: We wrote a custom hook to encapsulate the fetch-and-retry logic.

definition: Delaying a function's execution until a specified time has passed since its last invocation.
exampleSentence: Debounce the search input so it only fires after the user stops typing.

definition: A programming style that describes what the UI should look like rather than how to build it.
exampleSentence: React is declarative — you describe what the UI should look like, not how to build it.

definition: The second argument to React hooks that controls when an effect or memo should re-run.
exampleSentence: Add the user ID to the dependency array so the effect re-runs on change.

definition: A value computed from existing state rather than stored independently in the state tree.
exampleSentence: The total price is derived state — compute it from the cart items, don't store it.

definition: Document Object Model — the tree structure browsers use to represent and manipulate HTML.
exampleSentence: React updates the DOM efficiently by comparing the virtual tree first.

definition: Don't Repeat Yourself — a principle advocating for eliminating code duplication through abstraction.
exampleSentence: Extract that logic into a shared helper to keep the code DRY.

definition: Loading a JavaScript module on demand at runtime rather than at initial bundle load.
exampleSentence: A dynamic import loads the chart library only when the user opens that tab.

definition: An automated test that simulates real user scenarios across the full application stack.
exampleSentence: The end-to-end test simulates a real user completing the checkout flow.

definition: Attaching a single event listener to a parent element to handle events from its children.
exampleSentence: Event delegation handles all list item clicks from a single parent listener.

definition: The JavaScript runtime mechanism that processes the call stack and task queue sequentially.
exampleSentence: The event loop is why you can't block JavaScript with a long synchronous task.

definition: An alternative value, component, or behavior used when the primary option fails or is unavailable.
exampleSentence: Show a fallback skeleton while the data is still loading.

definition: A toggle that enables or disables a feature at runtime without deploying new code.
exampleSentence: The new checkout is behind a feature flag so we can enable it gradually.

definition: Creating a personal copy of a repository to develop independently from the original source.
exampleSentence: Fork the repo and open a pull request from your own branch.

definition: A reusable type definition in TypeScript that works with multiple types via a type parameter.
exampleSentence: A generic type lets the same hook work with strings, numbers, or objects.

definition: Automatically updating the browser with code changes without a full page refresh.
exampleSentence: Hot reload applies style changes instantly without losing the component state.

definition: Attaching React event listeners to server-rendered HTML to make it interactive on the client.
exampleSentence: Hydration errors happen when the server HTML doesn't match the client render.

definition: An operation that produces the same result regardless of how many times it is executed.
exampleSentence: The initialize function is idempotent — calling it twice has no extra effect.

definition: A pattern where data is never modified in place — changes produce new copies instead.
exampleSentence: Immutability makes state changes predictable and easy to trace in React.

definition: A browser API that efficiently detects when elements enter or leave the viewport.
exampleSentence: Intersection Observer triggers the animation when the section enters the viewport.

definition: Deferring the load of non-critical resources until they are actually needed by the user.
exampleSentence: Lazy loading images below the fold cut page weight by thirty percent.

definition: Moving shared state up to the nearest common ancestor component so siblings can access it.
exampleSentence: Lifting state to the parent let both sibling components read the same value.

definition: Automated static analysis that flags code style issues, errors, and anti-patterns.
exampleSentence: Linting catches unused imports and missing dependency arrays before code review.

definition: A CSS rule that applies styles conditionally based on device characteristics like screen width.
exampleSentence: A media query hides the sidebar on screens narrower than seven hundred pixels.

definition: Caching the result of a function call so repeated calls with the same input skip recomputation.
exampleSentence: Memoization prevents the expensive sort from running on every re-render.

definition: A function that intercepts and transforms requests or actions before they reach their destination.
exampleSentence: The router middleware checks authentication before rendering protected pages.

definition: A simulated object or function that replaces a real dependency during testing.
exampleSentence: Use a mock for the API in unit tests so they don't hit the real network.

definition: A single repository containing multiple related projects or packages managed together.
exampleSentence: All our packages live in a monorepo so teams can share components easily.

definition: Directly modifying existing data in memory rather than creating a new copy of it.
exampleSentence: Avoid direct mutation of state — always return a new object in a reducer.

definition: The ?? operator that returns the right-hand value only when the left is null or undefined.
exampleSentence: Use nullish coalescing to fall back to a default only when the value is null.

definition: The ?. operator that safely accesses nested properties without throwing if one is undefined.
exampleSentence: Optional chaining safely reads user?.profile?.avatar without crashing.

definition: Dividing a large dataset into discrete pages to reduce load time and improve usability.
exampleSentence: Pagination keeps the list fast by loading only twenty items at a time.

definition: Code that implements a modern feature in browsers that do not natively support it.
exampleSentence: A polyfill adds the missing method so older browsers can run the same code.

definition: A React feature that renders a child component into a DOM node outside the parent tree.
exampleSentence: A portal renders the modal at the body level so it escapes any overflow: hidden.

definition: Passing data through multiple component layers that don't use it to reach a deep child.
exampleSentence: Prop drilling five levels deep is a sign you should use context instead.

definition: A function with no side effects that always returns the same output for the same input.
exampleSentence: A pure function with the same inputs always returns exactly the same output.

definition: A bug where the outcome depends on the unpredictable timing of concurrent operations.
exampleSentence: A race condition caused the older response to overwrite the newer one.

definition: React's algorithm for comparing virtual DOM trees to determine the minimal DOM updates needed.
exampleSentence: React's reconciliation algorithm figures out the minimum DOM changes needed.

definition: Restructuring existing code to improve readability or structure without changing its behavior.
exampleSentence: We spent the sprint refactoring the form logic without changing any behavior.

definition: A React pattern where a component shares logic by accepting a function as a prop.
exampleSentence: The render prop pattern shares tooltip logic without imposing any specific UI.

definition: A React component updating its output in response to state or prop changes.
exampleSentence: Unnecessary re-renders were making the list feel sluggish on slow devices.

definition: JavaScript syntax using ... to collect remaining values into an array or expand iterables.
exampleSentence: Use the spread operator to merge the default props with the passed ones.

definition: The context that determines where variables are accessible within a program.
exampleSentence: The variable is out of scope because it was declared inside the if block.

definition: Converting a data structure into a format like JSON for storage or network transmission.
exampleSentence: Serialization converts the state object into a JSON string for storage.

definition: Generating full HTML on the server per request, improving initial load and SEO.
exampleSentence: Server-side rendering improves the first contentful paint on slow connections.

definition: Any operation a function performs beyond returning a value, such as a fetch or DOM mutation.
exampleSentence: Put the fetch call inside useEffect so the side effect runs after render.

definition: Storing a piece of data in one place so all parts of the app read from the same state.
exampleSentence: A single source of truth means the cart total is never out of sync.

definition: A CSS scoring system that determines which rule takes priority when multiple rules conflict.
exampleSentence: The inline style wins because it has the highest specificity score.

definition: Pre-rendering pages to static HTML at build time for fast delivery without a server.
exampleSentence: Static site generation pre-renders the blog so it loads without a server.

definition: A tool for developing and documenting UI components in isolation outside the main app.
exampleSentence: Open Storybook to see every button variant in isolation before using it.

definition: A React wrapper that intentionally double-invokes functions to surface side effect bugs.
exampleSentence: Strict mode exposed a side effect in our effect that only ran once before.

definition: Limiting a function to execute at most once per defined time interval regardless of calls.
exampleSentence: Throttle the scroll handler so it runs at most once every hundred milliseconds.

definition: A build optimization that removes unused exported code from the final bundle.
exampleSentence: Tree shaking removed the unused utility functions from the final bundle.

definition: TypeScript's ability to refine a broad type to a more specific one within a conditional block.
exampleSentence: Type narrowing inside the if block tells TypeScript the value is definitely a string.

definition: A TypeScript type representing a value that can be one of several specified types.
exampleSentence: A union type lets the prop accept either a string or a number.

definition: A React form element whose value is managed by the DOM rather than component state.
exampleSentence: An uncontrolled component reads its value from the DOM via a ref.

definition: An in-memory representation of the UI that React diffs against the real DOM before updating.
exampleSentence: The virtual DOM lets React batch updates and touch the real DOM as little as possible.

definition: A sequential chain of network requests where each one waits for the previous to complete.
exampleSentence: A request waterfall was adding two seconds to the page load time.

definition: Google's set of metrics measuring real-world user experience: LCP, INP, and CLS.
exampleSentence: We improved our Web Vitals score by lazy loading images and reducing layout shift.

definition: A minimal React state management library using a hook-based store without boilerplate.
exampleSentence: We switched to Zustand because the store is much simpler to set up than Redux.

definition: A React pattern where multiple components share implicit state to form a cohesive unit.
exampleSentence: A compound component like Tabs shares state between Tab and Panel internally.

definition: React's built-in mechanism for passing data through the component tree without prop drilling.
exampleSentence: The Context API passes the theme down without threading it through every component.

definition: A React component that catches JavaScript errors in its subtree and renders a fallback UI.
exampleSentence: An error boundary catches the crash and shows a friendly message instead.

definition: A bug where a function captures an outdated variable value from a previous render.
exampleSentence: A stale closure inside the timer was reading the count value from the first render.

definition: A parallel DOM representation that assistive technologies use to interpret the interface.
exampleSentence: The accessibility tree showed the button had no accessible name.

definition: Using HTML elements that convey meaning about content structure rather than just appearance.
exampleSentence: Use semantic HTML so screen readers and search engines understand the content.
